title: glechoma multifida
intro: Glechoma multifida, also known as Creeping Thyme or Wild Thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herb native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils, particularly thymol. The health benefits of Glechoma multifida include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce anxiety and stress, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for respiratory issues and skin conditions. Glechoma multifida is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can also be used in cooking and as a garnish for its distinctive flavor and aroma.
